CONTENT.Career: Cáseres made his professional debut for Vélez Sarsfield entering the field in a 0–3 defeat to Newell's Old Boys, for the 2016–17 Argentine Primera División. The team's coach at the time was Omar De Felippe.The midfielder became a regular starter for Vélez during the 2017–18 Argentine Primera División. Due to his strong performances, at the end of the first semester of the season Cáseres was linked with a possible move to Atlético Madrid, reportedly willing to pay his buyout clause of US$12 million.Cáseres signed a five-year contract with Villarreal CF on 16 July 2018.On 29 January 2020, Cáseres joined Liga MX side Club América on a one-year loan agreement up to December 31, 2020, with the option for a permanent deal.On 22 February 2021, Cáseres returned to Argentina to Primera División side Vélez Sarfield, on a loan deal until December 2021. After that, in 2022 Cáseres signed a contract with Vélez Sarfield until December 2024 for $2.36 Million.
CONTENT.External links: Profile at Vélez Sarsfield's official website (in Spanish)Santiago Cáseres at BDFA (in Spanish)Santiago Cáseres at Soccerway

SUMMARY: Santiago Cáseres (born 25 February 1997) is an Argentine professional footballer who plays as a defensive midfielder for Argentine club Vélez Sarfield.
